Chișinău apartment report
Listings (left) + Leaflet map (right). Click a listing to zoom/highlight the marker; click a marker to jump to the listing.
What you’re seeing: apartments for purchase (source-specific). Sector borders are approximate OSM admin relations. Late-evening busyness is a composite proxy within ~300m: food/drink density + transit density + culture venues + plaza/pedestrian-zone signal + pedestrian-friendly street length.
(filters)
Overlays (100m grid): each square shows OSM POI counts binned into a 100m×100m grid over Chișinău; color intensity is log-scaled density within that grid cell.
Map legend: pin confirmed pin unconfirmed pin mismatch
#1
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€169,950 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, 13/2
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare Deschis Adăugător Mobilat Încălzire autonomă Aparat de aer condiționat Interfon Ascensor Subcategoria Toate apartamente

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare Deschis Adăugător Mobilat Încălzire autonomă Aparat de aer condiționat Interfon Ascensor Subcategoria Toate apartamente
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Încălzire autonomă”
  • “3 camere decomandate”
  • “Suprafață totală: 96,6 mp”
  • “Apartament reparat și mobilat”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €169 950
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, 13/2

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Locație excelentă: în raza a 5 minute de mers pe jos se află grădiniță, școală, piață, supermarketuri (inclusiv Kaufland), bancă”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Ciocana”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Încălzire autonomă”
  • “Bloc nou cu ascensor”
  • “Suprafață totală: 89,82 mp”
  • “Se oferă spre vânzare un apartament modern și bine compartimentat, situat în sectorul Poșta Veche, pe strada Ceucari, în cadrul Complexului Cartierul Cluj, Bloc B04 (în construcție).”
  • “Aer condiționat”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €121 230
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Ceucari 14

Micro-area (local guide)

No specific landmarks/amenities are named in the ad text. Treat the map pin as approximate and verify walk times on foot.

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

#21
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€150,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Grenoble, 134/4
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • No strong seller emphasis could be reliably extracted from the text.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • Key building/usage details are not clearly specified in the ad text.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Complexul Solomon Grenoble Residence este construit într-o zonă verde,ecologică a sectorului Botanica, cu infrastructură nouă, școală, grădiniță, centru pentru tineret, spații comerciale;”
  • “Compania de construcții Solomon Costruct Imobil vă prezintă un proiect desăvârșit cu o concepție unică de apartamente cu 1, 2 și 3 camere în complexul locativ Solomon Grenoble Residence.”
  • “Ușa metalică de clasa Premim, 3 contururi de etanșare, termoizolată cu vată minerală, tocul termoizolat, placată cu panouri MDF pe ambele părți;”
  • “Structura din beton monolit (grinzi, coloane, diafragme și planșee), piloni de 300 mm, planșee 200 mm, constructiv ce asigură stabilitate ridicată, nivel redus de vibrații și zgomot.”
  • “Geamurile vor avea următoarele caracteristici de bază: -Panoramice;- Profil PVC cu 5 camere, seria 72 și în sus(în dependență de tipul confecției);- Termopan cu 3 sticle, cu efect special(eficiență energetică);”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €150 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Grenoble, 134/4

  • “Structura din beton monolit (grinzi, coloane, diafragme și planșee), piloni de 300 mm, planșee 200 mm, constructiv ce asigură stabilitate ridicată, nivel redus de vibrații și zgomot.”

  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentare: 3 camere, bucătărie, 2 blocuri sanitare, verandă”
  • “Suprafață totală: 81,81 mp”
  • “Vânzare Apartament 3 Camere, 81.81 mp – Complex Smart Home, Râșcani”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €104 717
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Calea Moșilor 4

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Aproape de parc, grădiniță, școală și cafenele”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Râșcani”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare subterană și spații comerciale.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare subterană și spații comerciale.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Proiectul este constituit din 2 blocuri locative a câte 12 etaje, 3 nivele de parcare subterană și spații comerciale.”
  • “Compania de construcții Estate Invest Company oferă spre vânzare apartamente în complexul locativ Estate Sunrise III, amplasat pe strada Ialoveni 134, sectorul Telecentru.”
  • “Geamuri cu 3 rânduri de sticlă;”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Sector (from the listing title): “Centru”.

#47
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€259,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · bd. Decebal, 99/B
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • No strong seller emphasis could be reliably extracted from the text.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • Key building/usage details are not clearly specified in the ad text.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Apartamentul e compartimentat din: bucătăria comasată cu livingul, 3 dormitoare, 2 blocuri sanitare și garderobă.”
  • “Apartament spațios cu 3 camere și living cu reparație premium după designer proiect, fiind mobilat și dotat cu toată tehnica necesară.”
  • “Complexul ,, Decebal Avenue Residence “ amplasat în mun.”
  • “Electricitatea e proiectată după ultimele standarde, încălzire prin pardosea toată suprafața apartamentului.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €259 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): bd. Decebal, 99/B

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Electricitatea e proiectată după ultimele standarde, încălzire prin pardosea toată suprafața apartamentului.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Botanica”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €89 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Salcâmilor, .

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Botanica”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • No strong seller emphasis could be reliably extracted from the text.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • Key building/usage details are not clearly specified in the ad text.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Albișoara, 82/8 Contacte :”

  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Suprafață: 85 m² et.”
  • “Reparație nouă, mobilat”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Botanica”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare Deschis Adăugător Gata de mutat Mobilat Cu tehnică electrocasnică Încălzire autonomă Aparat de aer condiționat Geamuri

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare Deschis Adăugător Gata de mutat Mobilat Cu tehnică electrocasnică Încălzire autonomă Aparat de aer condiționat Geamuri
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “1 dormitor, living, bucătărie, balcon, grup sanitar și antreu.”
  • “1 dormitor, grup sanitar, garderobă.”
  • “Spre vânzare un apartament cu 3 odai, compartimenat în 2 nivele:”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • “Locuința are o suprafață de 110,36 m2, localizat la etajul 4 din 4, fiind compartimentat în: 3 camere, bucătărie, living, terasă, 2 blocuri sanitare, antreu, apartamentele pot fi achitate și in rate de la dezvoltator.”
  • “Complex cu Blocuri Locative cu 4 Etaje cu ascensor.”
  • “Complexul este construit pe o suprafață generoasă de 1,04 hectare, format din 7 blocuri cu un total de doar 200 apartamente clasa premium.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €109 808
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Ialoveni 100

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “La o distanță de 350 metri se regăsesc diverse magazine alimentare și de uz casnic, sucursale bancare, farmacii, restaurante, grădiniță, școală.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Centru”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • If the pin is not confirmed, treat nearby-amenity context as approximate and verify the exact micro-location.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

#80
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€260,000 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, 15
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare Deschis Subcategoria Toate apartamentele Preț : 260 000 € Regiunea : Chișinău mun., Chișinău, Ciocana, bd. Mircea cel B

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare Deschis Subcategoria Toate apartamentele Preț : 260 000 € Regiunea : Chișinău mun., Chișinău, Ciocana, bd. Mircea cel B
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Temperatură excelentă: la -9°C afară, în apartament sunt ~19°C fără încălzire”
  • “Suprafață: 97 mp”
  • “Etajul 14 din 21”
  • “Încălzire autonomă”
  • “Se vinde complet mobilat și utilat.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, 15

No specific landmarks/amenities are named in the ad text. Treat the map pin as approximate and verify walk times on foot.

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Apartamentul este gata de trai cu un design individual modern, compartimentat în 2 dormitoare și salon spațios cu bucătărie.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €289 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Liviu Deleanu, 10/18

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Apartament într-o zonă liniștită, cu acces facil la mijloacele de transport public în orice direcție a orașului.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Buiucani”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentare reușită: 2 dormitoare separate, salon spațios, bucătărie, balcon, bloc sanitar, antreu.”
  • “Încălzire autonomă, geamuri panoramice, vedere frumoasă!”
  • “Dispune de reparație de calitate, este complet mobilat și dotat cu toată tehnica pentru uz casnic de calitate.”
  • “Se vinde apartament superb, absolut nou, în complexul rezidențial realizat de compania ExFactor, pe str.”
  • “Bunul imobil, cu suprafața de 74 m.p., este poziționat la etajul 9 din 12 cu vedere frumoasă spre pădure.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €204 900
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Ion Buzdugan

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Alba-Iulia, piața Delfin, supermarkete, centre comerciale, farmacie, bancă, școală etc.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Buiucani”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

#100
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Ion Buzdugan, 13
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare subterană, deschisă;

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare subterană, deschisă;
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentare: antreu, 3 dormitoare, bucătărie + living, 2 blocuri sanitare, balcon.”
  • “încălzire autonomă;”
  • “Apartament cu o suprafață de 87 mp, amplasat pe strada Ion Buzdugan 13, Buiucani.”
  • “bloc nou (ExFactor);”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €185 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Ion Buzdugan, 13

Micro-area (local guide)

No specific landmarks/amenities are named in the ad text. Treat the map pin as approximate and verify walk times on foot.

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • If the pin is not confirmed, treat nearby-amenity context as approximate and verify the exact micro-location.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Locuința se prezintă cu reparație la cel mai înalt nivel, are suprafața de 88m2 și este compartimentată în: 3 camere, living, bucătărie, balcon, blocuri sanitare și antreu!”
  • “* încălzire autonomă;”
  • “ATENȚIE!Acum poți cumpăra casa în credit cu doar 30% rată inițială!”
  • “* euroreparație;”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

#114
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€420,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. București, 26
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare gratuite. Acces limitat cu supraveghere video, concierge service.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare gratuite. Acces limitat cu supraveghere video, concierge service.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentat în: antreu, dormitor matrimonial cu garderobă și o baie spațioasă și balcon francez, al 2-lea dormitor cu balcon francez, baie, salon cu bucătărie spațios cu acces la terasă.”
  • “Complexul locativ THE #1, amplasat în Centru, pe strada București 26, vizavi de stadionul Republican unde va fi noul sediu al Ambasadei SUA.”
  • “Încălzire autonomă, cazan + boiler.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Totodatî complexul dispune și de locuri de parcare subterană dar și debarale!”
  • “Compania de construcții ESTATE INVEST COMPANY oferă spre procurare apartamente în complexul locativ ESTATE BUCOVINA , situat în sectorul Ciocana la intersecția a 3 cele mai importante străzi (str.”
  • “ESTATE BUCOVINA este un complex locativ constituit din 2 tranșe.”
  • “Sau la oficiul nostru de vânzări care este amplasat lângă complex pe strada Bucovina 1.”
  • “Ansamblu locativ este amplasat într-o zonă de dezvoltare continuă la doar 2 minute de PORT MALL.”
  • “Aici puteți procura apartamente în rate pe termen de pînă la 4 ani, cu doar 10% prima rată, fără implicarea băncilor.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Bucovinei, 1 B

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare subterană.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare subterană.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentare: trei dormitoare, living, bucătărie, antreu, bloc sanitar separat, balcon.”
  • “Imobilul, cu o suprafață de 71 m.p., este poziționat la etajul 2 din 13.”
  • “Spre vânzare apartament cu trei camere și living, într-un bloc nou, situat în sectorul Buiucani, strada Vasile Lupu.”
  • “: design individual, finisaje de calitate superioară, obiecte sanitare instalate, mobilier realizat la comandă, dotat cu tehnică de uz casnic performantă, geamuri termopane, încălzire autonomă, pardoseală încălzită.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €185 000
  • Rooms: 4
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Vasile Lupu

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “În imediată apropiere se găsesc numeroase puncte sociale și comerciale: supermarket, farmacie, bancă, grădiniță, sală de fitness, universitate, colegiu, spital etc.”
  • “: design individual, finisaje de calitate superioară, obiecte sanitare instalate, mobilier realizat la comandă, dotat cu tehnică de uz casnic performantă, geamuri termopane, încălzire autonomă, pardoseală încălzită.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Buiucani”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €110 500
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Regina Elisabeta, 2

Micro-area (local guide)

No specific landmarks/amenities are named in the ad text. Treat the map pin as approximate and verify walk times on foot.

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Centru”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentare: 3 dormitoare, living spațios, bucătărie, 2 blocuri sanitare, balcon.”
  • “încălzire autonomă;”
  • “Apartament cu o suprafață de 152 mp, amplasat pe strada Hristo Botev 6, Botanica.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €370 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Hristo Botev, 6

Micro-area (local guide)

No specific landmarks/amenities are named in the ad text. Treat the map pin as approximate and verify walk times on foot.

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Botanica”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€255,000 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, 41
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • No strong seller emphasis could be reliably extracted from the text.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • Key building/usage details are not clearly specified in the ad text.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Vind apartament cu 3 camere spatios reparatie executata cu designer, apartametul nu a fost facut pentru vinzare si sa investit bine in el”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €255 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, 41

Micro-area (local guide)

No specific landmarks/amenities are named in the ad text. Treat the map pin as approximate and verify walk times on foot.

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Ciocana”.

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Râșcani”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare deschisă.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare deschisă.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentare: două dormitoare, living, bucătărie, baie, antreu, balcon.”
  • “și este poziționat la etajul 4 din 5.”
  • “: euroreparație, finisaje de calitate superioară, mobilat și utilat cu tehnică casnică, geamuri termopan, orientare bilaterală.”
  • “Caracteristici: euroreparație, finisaje de calitate superioară, mobilat și utilat cu tehnică casnică, geamuri termopan, orientare bilaterală.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): strada Independenței

  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Râșcani”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: parcare Deschis Adăugător Gata de mutat Mobilat Cu tehnică electrocasnică Încălzire prin pardoseală Interfon Ascensor Teren de

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: parcare Deschis Adăugător Gata de mutat Mobilat Cu tehnică electrocasnică Încălzire prin pardoseală Interfon Ascensor Teren de
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Suprafață: 72 mp”
  • “Încălzire centralizată”
  • “Bucătăria este complet dotată cu tehnică, astfel încât să te poți muta fără investiții suplimentare.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.

  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).

  • “Spre vânzare apartament cu două dormitoare și living, bloc nou, amplasat în sectorul Poșta Veche, strada Iazului.”
  • “Imobilul, cu o suprafață de 75 m.p., este situat la etajul 8 din 13.”
  • “: design individual, finisaje de calitate superioară, obiecte sanitare instalate, mobilier realizat la comandă, dotat cu tehnică de uz casnic performantă, geamuri termopane, încălzire autonomă.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €169 900
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): strada Iazului

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “În imediată apropiere se găsesc numeroase puncte sociale și comerciale: supermarket, farmacie, bancă, grădiniță, sală de fitness, universitate, colegiu, spital etc.”
  • “: design individual, finisaje de calitate superioară, obiecte sanitare instalate, mobilier realizat la comandă, dotat cu tehnică de uz casnic performantă, geamuri termopane, încălzire autonomă.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.
