#174
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€109,900 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Muncesti
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • parking: Parcare de tip deschis, teren de joacă pentru copii.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • parking: Parcare de tip deschis, teren de joacă pentru copii.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

What is explicitly stated (with quotes):

  • “Compartimentat în: 3 dormitoare, bucătărie, 2 blocuri sanitare, 2 balcoane, debara, antreu.”
  • “Comision 0% pentru cumpărător”
  • “Muncești, suprafața totală de 674 mp.”

What’s missing / verify (not stated):

  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded
  • Monthly maintenance / building admin fees
  • Winter heating costs (proof / bills)
  • Sun exposure / orientation
  • Noise (arterial road / courtyard)

Viewing checklist (practical):

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €109 900
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): str. Muncesti

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Еste în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

  • Ask for the cadastral extract and verify the listed area matches what is sold.
  • Clarify what stays (furniture/appliances) vs what is excluded — put it in writing.
  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
FACTS BOX (from listing fields / ad)
  • Price: €239 000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Address (needs pin check): bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, 39

Micro-area (local guide)

Nearby amenities mentioned in the ad:

  • “Bloc nou, Amplasare reușită aproape de marketuri, farmacii, școli și transport public.”

On-the-ground checks (5–10 minutes each):

  • Walk the exact route to your daily errands (grocery/pharmacy) and note sidewalk quality and lighting.
  • Check evening noise and parking pressure (weekday 20:00–22:00, weekend).
  • If the building is on/near a boulevard, listen for traffic with windows open.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from the listing title): “Ciocana”.

General local context (not source-verified): In Chișinău, comfort varies block-by-block even within the same sector. Prioritize (1) building condition and yard upkeep, (2) road noise, (3) practical access to trolleybus/bus routes, and (4) proximity to everyday services rather than “prestige” claims.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

A) What the seller leans on:

  • No strong seller emphasis could be reliably extracted from the text.

B) Stated vs not stated (be strict):

  • Explicitly stated:
  • Key building/usage details are not clearly specified in the ad text.
  • Not stated:
  • Exact floor level / storey
  • Elevator / lift
  • Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden)
  • Parking / garage availability and terms
  • Energy certificate figures (HWB / fGEE)
  • Tenancy status (vacant vs rented, end date)
  • Furnishing included/excluded

C) Grounded read-between-the-lines:

  • The ad is light on some buyer-critical specifics; treat the viewing as a fact-finding mission and request documents.

D) Viewing questions (bring these):

  • Which floor is it on, and is there a working elevator? (If yes: from which level?)
  • What exactly is included in the monthly costs (utilities, maintenance, building admin), and are there any planned increases?
  • Is the apartment vacant on handover, or is there an existing tenancy/occupant?
  • What is included in the sale (furniture/appliances), and what is excluded?
  • Are there upcoming building works (roof/facade/risers) and what is the reserve fund status (if applicable)?
  • If renovation/first-occupancy language is used: what scope is completed and what is still pending? Can you provide a written spec?
  • If parking is mentioned: is it included, optional, or just nearby? What are the terms and costs?

  • Check heating: type, last winter bills, and service history.
  • If there is an elevator/parking claim, verify access and documents (rights/lease).
  • Do a 10-minute noise test with windows open (day + evening).
